title: Ethical Transition, Rejection Learning, and Continuing Career Development · Opportunity Transfer Studio
summary: A different candidate, role family, location, access need, employment history, constraint, and hiring process requires an independent transfer of ethical transition, rejection learning, and continuing career development into a changed first-90-days and continuing career evidence plan. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of first-90-days and continuing career evidence plan with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, ch…
objectives: Close accepted, declined, rejected, withdrawn, and expired opportunities with concise communication, returned property, retained records, and privacy-safe deletion.
Separate direct feedback, process outcome, missing signal, candidate inference, repeated pattern, and uncontrollable variance before changing strategy.
Honor notice, confidentiality, intellectual property, client, account, device, access, data, and relationship duties with qualified local review where needed.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Close Process
steps.instruction:
lab case — A candidate interprets sparse rejection as a skill verdict, retains confidential work, closes relationships poorly, and enters a new role without an onboarding evidence plan. The lab variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Close Process.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for close accepted, declined, rejected, withdrawn, and expired opportunities with concise communication, returned property, retained records, and privacy-safe deletion. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Close Process, keep Close Process connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Career Evidence Loop
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Career Evidence Loop: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A candidate interprets sparse rejection as a skill verdict, retains confidential work, closes relationships poorly, and enters a new role without an onboarding evidence plan. The lab variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Maintain a consented private evidence log, retrieve skills, seek feedback, update public proof selectively, and review direction without exposing confidential work.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Lab Career Evidence Loop.
steps.why: Retrieving Career Evidence Loop through the concrete first-90-days and continuing career evidence plan case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Career Evidence Loop, keep Career Evidence Loop connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Maintain a consented private evidence log, retrieve skills, seek feedback, update public proof selectively, and review direction without exposing confidential work.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Lab Career Evidence Loop, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Career Evidence Loop precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Lab Career Evidence Loop, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A candidate interprets sparse rejection as a skill verdict, retains confidential work, closes relationships poorly, and enters a new role without an onboarding evidence plan. The lab variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Maintain a consented private evidence log, retrieve skills, seek feedback, update public proof selectively, and review direction without exposing confidential work.

For Defend the transferred model · Lab Career Evidence Loop, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Career development happens only during the next job search.
